rule: Rules are to be interpreted in order of precedence from Rule 1 (most general) to Rule 6 (most specific); a service must satisfy all applicable lower-numbered rules to qualify under a higher-numbered rule.

"Public launch" means that Tesla makes the robotaxi service available to any member of the general public to summon a ride via a publicly accessible app or reservation system, and at least one such ride is completed by a member of the general public before the deadline.

"Credible reporting" refers to independently verifiable reports from reputable news outlets, regulatory filings, or official Tesla communications; a consensus is reached when at least two such sources independently confirm the occurrence of the event.

This market resolves to 'Yes' if, by June 30, 2024 11:59 PM ET, Tesla publicly launches a fully driverless robotaxi service that meets the definitions in Meta-Rules 2-3; otherwise it resolves to 'No'.

A qualifying service is one that allows any member of the general public to summon and ride in a Tesla vehicle that operates without any human - onboard or remote - actively controlling the vehicle during normal operation.

Human presence for emergency monitoring is permitted, provided the human is not physically positioned to take control (e.g., no safety driver in the driver's seat) and does not actively steer, brake, accelerate, or otherwise drive the vehicle except in genuine emergency interventions.

Services limited to Tesla employees, invite-only testers, closed-beta participants, factory self-delivery, or the mere release of Full Self-Driving software for private owner-drivers do not satisfy the public launch requirement.
Possession of regulatory permits, press demonstrations, prototype unveilings, or any testing without live public ridership does not constitute a public launch.
The market's resolution will be determined based on a consensus of credible reporting as defined in Meta-Rule 3.
